Article.ArticleTitle: The Impact of Regular Physical Activity on Children's Health Parameters: A Critical Literature Analysis of Metabolism, the Musculoskeletal System, Respiratory System Diseases, and Neuropsychiatry.

Abstract.AbstractText: Physical activity is widely recognized as beneficial to children's health, but knowledge about the positive effects depending on the intensity and regularity of physical exercise remains insufficient. This study aimed to compile research covering a broad pediatric population and identify the most significant, commonly observed benefits, as well as to analyze detailed research results concerning the most common health disorders, such as obesity, diabetes, and respiratory system diseases. It has been shown that the greater the regularity and intensity of exercise, the better the health parameters observed in children. The benefits of physical training also include improved subjective well-being, greater self-esteem, better emotional regulation, and overall improvement in cognitive function. They increase bone mineral content and mineral density, which leads to increased strength and stability of the skeleton. Habits formed during childhood have a significant impact on the potential development of diseases in adulthood; therefore, interdisciplinary cooperation between specialists is advisable, and, in certain cases, the preparation of individual exercise interventions, especially in the case of coexisting endocrine disorders.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Conflicts of interest: In compliance with the ICMJE uniform disclosure form, all authors declare the following: Payment/services info: All authors have declared that no financial support was received from any organization for the submitted work. Financial relationships: All authors have declared that they have no financial relationships at present or within the previous three years with any organizations that might have an interest in the submitted work. Other relationships: All authors have declared that there are no other relationships or activities that could appear to have influenced the submitted work.

Reference.Citation: Systematic review of the health benefits of physical activity and fitness in school-aged children and youth. Janssen I, Leblanc AG. Int J Behav Nutr Phys Act. 2010;7:40.

Substituting prolonged sedentary time and cardiovascular risk in children and youth: a meta-analysis within the International Children's Accelerometry database (ICAD) Wijndaele K, White T, Andersen LB, et al. Int J Behav Nutr Phys Act. 2019;16:96.

Physical activity and health in children younger than 6 years: a systematic review. Pate RR, Hillman CH, Janz KF, Katzmarzyk PT, Powell KE, Torres A, Whitt-Glover MC. Med Sci Sports Exerc. 2019;51:1282–1291.

The effects of exercise training on insulin resistance in children and adolescents with overweight or obesity: a systematic review and meta-analysis. Kazeminasab F, Sharafifard F, Miraghajani M, Behzadnejad N, Rosenkranz SK. Front Endocrinol (Lausanne) 2023;14:1178376.
Why should the next generation of youth guidelines prioritize vigorous physical activity? Chaabene H, Markov A, Schega L. Sports Med Open. 2024;10:87.
Systematic review of the relationships between physical activity and health indicators in the early years (0-4 years) Carson V, Lee EY, Hewitt L, et al. BMC Public Health. 2017;17:854.

High-intensity activity is more strongly associated with metabolic health in children compared to sedentary time: a cross-sectional study of the I.Family cohort. Fridolfsson J, Buck C, Hunsberger M, et al. Int J Behav Nutr Phys Act. 2021;18:90.

Physical activity and education about physical activity for chronic musculoskeletal pain in children and adolescents. Nascimento Leite M, Kamper SJ, O'Connell NE, et al. Cochrane Database Syst Rev. 2023;7:0.

Systematic review of the relationships between objectively measured physical activity and health indicators in school-aged children and youth. Poitras VJ, Gray CE, Borghese MM, et al. Appl Physiol Nutr Metab. 2016;41:0–239.
Association between parent and child physical activity: a systematic review. Petersen TL, Møller LB, Brønd JC, Jepsen R, Grøntved A. Int J Behav Nutr Phys Act. 2020;17:67.
Physical activity in adolescents and children and relationship to metabolic health. Whooten R, Kerem L, Stanley T. Curr Opin Endocrinol Diabetes Obes. 2019;26:25–31.
Exercise management in type 1 diabetes: a consensus statement. Riddell MC, Gallen IW, Smart CE, et al. Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol. 2017;5:377–390.

Exercise and insulin resistance markers in children and adolescents with excess weight: a systematic review and network meta-analysis. García-Hermoso A, López-Gil JF, Izquierdo M, Ramírez-Vélez R, Ezzatvar Y. JAMA Pediatr. 2023;177:1276–1284.

Exercise, adipokines and pediatric obesity: a meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials. García-Hermoso A, Ceballos-Ceballos RJ, Poblete-Aro CE, Hackney AC, Mota J, Ramírez-Vélez R. Int J Obes (Lond) 2017;41:475–482.
Physical activity, exercise, and mental health of healthy adolescents: a review of the last 5 ​years. Ruiz-Ranz E, Asín-Izquierdo I. Sports Med Health Sci. 2025;7:161–172.

The effects of physical activity on the mental health of typically developing children and adolescents: a systematic review and meta-analysis. Fu Q, Li L, Li Q, Wang J. BMC Public Health. 2025;25:1514.
Impact of physical activity on symptoms of depression and anxiety in adolescents during the COVID-19 pandemic. Yang HY, Sun LH. Front Psychiatry. 2025;16:1631978.
Effects of movement behaviors on preschoolers' cognition: a systematic review of randomized controlled trials. Pacheco C, Culkin V, Putkaradze A, Zeng N. Int J Behav Nutr Phys Act. 2025;22:12.
The effects of physical exercise on cognitive function in adolescents: a systematic review and meta-analysis. Liu L, Xin X, Zhang Y. Front Psychol. 2025;16:1556721.

Exercise and cardiovascular disease risk factors in children and adolescents with obesity: a systematic review with meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials. Kelley GA, Kelley KS, Pate RR. Am J Lifestyle Med. 2022;16:485–510.

Cardiorespiratory fitness and health in children and adolescents: an overview of systematic reviews with meta-analyses representing over 125 000 observations covering 33 health-related outcomes. Demchenko I, Prince SA, Merucci K, et al. Br J Sports Med. 2025;59:856–865.

Effects of aerobic exercise and resistance exercise on physical indexes and cardiovascular risk factors in obese and overweight school-age children: a systematic review and meta-analysis. Chen T, Lin J, Lin Y, et al. PLoS One. 2021;16:0.

The effectiveness of physical activity interventions on blood pressure in children and adolescents: a systematic review and network meta-analysis. Hassan MA, Zhou W, Ye M, He H, Gao Z. J Sport Health Sci. 2024;13:699–708.

Effects of exercise on bone mass and bone metabolism in adolescents: a systematic review and meta-analysis. Zhang W, Wang X, Liu Y, He Q, Ding Q, Mei J, Li X. Front Physiol. 2024;15:1512822.

Musculoskeletal benefits from a physical activity program in primary school are retained 4 years after the program is terminated. Rosengren BE, Lindgren E, Jehpsson L, Dencker M, Karlsson MK. Calcif Tissue Int. 2021;109:405–414.
The role of obesity in pediatric orthopedics. Nowicki P, Kemppainen J, Maskill L, Cassidy J. J Am Acad Orthop Surg Glob Res Rev. 2019;3:0.
